# Project Security & Architecture Map
*(Auto-generated & continuously updated by WebSec Brief Agent)*

## 1. Identity & Stack
: Node.js runtime with Express.js web framework. TypeScript/JavaScript backend with Angular frontend. Monolithic architecture with clear separation between frontend (Angular) and backend (Express/Node.js). Project structure includes standard MVC patterns with models, routes, lib utilities, and configuration files.

## 2. Deployment & Runtime
: Startup Command: `npm start` runs `node build/app`. Entrypoint: server.ts (compiles to build/app.js). Configuration: config/default.yml for application settings, challenges, products, and features. Build Steps: `npm run build:frontend` (Angular build) and `npm run build:server` (TypeScript compilation). Postinstall script handles frontend dependencies and builds. Supports Docker, Vagrant, and packaged distributions.

## 3. Routing
: Express.js routing paradigm with RESTful API design. Key route files located in routes/ directory (login.ts, basket.ts, order.ts, etc.). Static assets served from /frontend/dist, /ftp, /encryptionkeys, and /support/logs directories. Directory browsing enabled on /ftp and /support/logs using serve-index middleware. API endpoints prefixed with /api/. Frontend routes handled by Angular SPA.

## 4. Security Mechanics
: JWT-based authentication using jsonwebtoken library. Role-based authorization (admin, deluxe, customer) via custom middleware. Intentionally vulnerable input validation including SQL injection (routes/login.ts), XSS, and other OWASP Top Ten issues. Security headers partially implemented via helmet but intentionally misconfigured for training purposes. Rate limiting on some endpoints. CORS enabled broadly. Security.txt and metrics endpoints exposed.

## 5. Data Layer
: SQLite database (primary) with Sequelize ORM for data access. Models defined in models/ directory (UserModel, ProductModel, BasketModel, etc.). Database initialization and relations configured in models/index.ts. Supports also MongoDB via MarsDB for NoSQL challenges. Raw SQL queries used in some routes for intentional vulnerability demonstration.

## Limitations
: Exact runtime vulnerability mapping, complete list of 100+ intentional security flaws, dynamic challenge flag configurations, full frontend build pipeline details, CI/CD workflow specifics, cloud deployment configurations (Heroku, AWS), and complete API endpoint documentation. Some route handlers and middleware logic truncated in provided source snippets.
